Class: EvSmartRoutingResponse

bemap.EvSmartRoutingResponse(options)

new EvSmartRoutingResponse(options)

BeNomad BeMap JavaScript API - EvSmartRoutingResponse (v1.5) Strongly-typed response from `POST service/evsmartrouting/1.0`. Wire shape mirrors `EvSmartRoutingResponse` on the server side (`bemap_idea/.../service/v1_0_0/model/evSmartRouting/response/EvSmartRoutingResponse.java`): { logTag, journey: { distance, duration, batteryLevel, consumed, chargingTime, chargingCost, departureTime, arrivalTime, vehicle, savedCo2Emissions }, inputInfo, boundingBox, route: { stepPoints, polyline?, encodedPolyline?, events?, timeSlotWarns? } }
Parameters:
Name Type Description
options Object Parsed JSON response from the server.
Source:
  • bemap-services-v2/ev-routing/bemap-ev-smart-routing-response.js, line 23

Methods

(static) fromJson(json) → {bemap.EvSmartRoutingResponse}

Parse the raw JSON response into a `bemap.EvSmartRoutingResponse`.
Parameters:
Name Type Description
json Object
Source:
  • bemap-services-v2/ev-routing/bemap-ev-smart-routing-response.js, line 149
Returns:
Type
bemap.EvSmartRoutingResponse

getArrivalBatteryLevel() → {Number}

Remaining battery level (percent, 0-100) at the end of the trip.
Source:
  • bemap-services-v2/ev-routing/bemap-ev-smart-routing-response.js, line 119
Returns:
Type
Number

getBoundingBox() → {bemap.BoundingBox|null}

`bemap.BoundingBox` covering the whole journey, or `null` when absent. `fromJson` already lifts `{topLeft, bottomRight}` to a `bemap.BoundingBox`, so under normal conditions this returns a `bemap.BoundingBox`. If the BoundingBox lift failed (e.g. malformed payload), the raw JSON object is returned so callers can still inspect it.
Source:
  • bemap-services-v2/ev-routing/bemap-ev-smart-routing-response.js, line 49
Returns:
Type
bemap.BoundingBox | null

getChargingTime() → {Number}

Total time spent charging across all stops, in seconds.
Source:
  • bemap-services-v2/ev-routing/bemap-ev-smart-routing-response.js, line 110
Returns:
Type
Number

getConsumed() → {Number}

Total energy consumed across the whole journey, in kWh.
Source:
  • bemap-services-v2/ev-routing/bemap-ev-smart-routing-response.js, line 128
Returns:
Type
Number

getDistance() → {Number}

Distance of the whole journey, in metres.
Source:
  • bemap-services-v2/ev-routing/bemap-ev-smart-routing-response.js, line 92
Returns:
`0` when the journey is missing.
Type
Number

getDuration() → {Number}

Duration of the whole journey, in seconds (including charging stops).
Source:
  • bemap-services-v2/ev-routing/bemap-ev-smart-routing-response.js, line 101
Returns:
`0` when the journey is missing.
Type
Number

getEncodedPolyline() → {String|null}

Google-encoded polyline string, when the request asked for `encodedPolyline`.
Source:
  • bemap-services-v2/ev-routing/bemap-ev-smart-routing-response.js, line 83
Returns:
Type
String | null

getPolyline() → {Array.<bemap.Coordinate>}

Route polyline as an Array of `bemap.Coordinate`, or empty when the server omitted the polyline (e.g. `pl=false` in the request).
Source:
  • bemap-services-v2/ev-routing/bemap-ev-smart-routing-response.js, line 69
Returns:
Type
Array.<bemap.Coordinate>

getSavedCo2Emissions() → {Number|null}

Saved CO2 emissions (kg), when `co2Emissions` was set in the request.
Source:
  • bemap-services-v2/ev-routing/bemap-ev-smart-routing-response.js, line 137
Returns:
Type
Number | null

getStepPoints() → {Array.<bemap.EvStepPoint>}

Charging stops in order along the journey, parsed into `EvStepPoint`.
Source:
  • bemap-services-v2/ev-routing/bemap-ev-smart-routing-response.js, line 58
Returns:
Type
Array.<bemap.EvStepPoint>
